categories_empty_text: Create your first category to classify content and feed the site filters.
categories_help_intro: Create source categories, attach them to the right module, then complete translations before using them across the site.
categories_help_step_create: Start by creating a source category with a clean name and slug.
categories_help_step_module: Then choose the correct attached module to keep the taxonomy consistent.
categories_help_step_translate: Finally add translations when the category must exist in several languages.
categories_tour_form_create_next_content: Save the source category first, then complete any missing translations before using it in posts or other content.
categories_tour_form_edit_next_content: Save your changes, review the related module and active status, then check the translations before returning to the content that uses this category.
categories_tour_form_editor_content: This area contains the editorial fields for the active category: name, slug, and description.
categories_tour_form_fields_content: Fill in the name, slug, and description for the active language here. Take care of the source language first, because it becomes the base for all other variants.
categories_tour_form_settings_content: Choose the related module and the active or inactive status of the category here. Validate these settings before assigning the category to content so the taxonomy stays clean.
categories_tour_form_sidebar_content: The right column links the category to its content module and controls whether it is active.
categories_tour_form_translations_conten: These tabs are used to translate the name, slug, and description properly. Always start with the source, save it, then adapt the other languages.
categories_tour_list_batch_content: Use this block to process several categories at once when reorganizing a module or a locale.
categories_tour_list_empty_content: Start by creating a clear source category in your main language. Once it is saved, you can open its translations and use it in posts or other related modules.
categories_tour_list_ready_next_content: Open an existing category now to review its source label, translations, and assigned module before linking it to new content.
categories_tour_list_table_content: The list shows the name, available languages, parent module, and active or inactive state for each category.
categories_tour_list_toolbar_content: Create a category, open the trash, and keep a quick view of your taxonomy organization.
confirm_delete_categories_batch: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the selected categories?
name: Name
translation_module_follow_source: This translation follows the source category module.
translation_save_first: Save the category first to add its translations.
translation_status_follow_source: This translation follows the activation state of the source category.
